article_title: WordPress 7.0 Ships May 20 Without Real-Time Collaboration After Matt Mullenweg Pulls the Phase 3 Flagship 12 Days Before Release
summary: Thoroughly and accurately sourced News report on the RTC cut from WordPress 7.0, with verbatim quotes and a substantive performance-analysis source the weaker sibling (#1265) lacks. The only finding is reputable WordPress-project and community domains not yet on the allowlist, which is not a content defect.

content_preview.title: WordPress 7.0 Ships May 20 Without Real-Time Collaboration After Matt Mullenweg Pulls the Phase 3 Flagship 12 Days Before Release
content_preview.summary: WordPress 7.0 arrives May 20 with new blocks, an AI Connectors API, and a redesigned admin, but its headline collaboration feature was cut on May 8 over performance and reliability concerns.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

WordPress 7.0 is scheduled for release on May 20, 2026, according to the [WordPress.org roadmap](https://wordpress.org/about/roadmap/), but it will ship without the feature that had been its defining goal. On May 8, 2026, the WordPress Core team announced that [real-time collaboration will not ship in WordPress 7.0](https://make.wordpress.org/core/2026/05/08/rtc-removed-from-7-0/), removing the feature 12 days before the planned release. The post, written by contributor annezazu, st...

recommendations: Consider adding the official WordPress project domains (wordpress.org, make.wordpress.org, developer.wordpress.org) and gutenbergtimes.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-organized News piece (939 words). Clear structure: the decision and rationale, the performance-testing analysis behind it, what still ships, what's unknown, and an Analysis section on the timing. The performance-analysis detail (four storage strategies, the 52%-faster finding, the 26ms pathological path) gives this piece analytical depth the colliding submission #1265 lacks.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 source snapshots read from disk and verified, all HTTP 200. source-0.html.gz (WordPress.org roadmap): confirms May 20 2026 / 7.1 Aug 19 / 7.2 Dec 10 dates, the Phase 3 'Collaboration — A more intuitive way to co-author content' definition verbatim, and the 'advancing Phase 3 collaboration features, client-side media handling...' quote verbatim. source-1.html.gz (Make Core 'RTC will not ship'): confirms author annezazu, '@matt made the decision to remove real-time collaboration from WordPress 7.0' verbatim, 'not confident the current approach is robust enough to include in Core' verbatim, the 'surface area, race conditions, server load, memory efficiency, and recurring bugs found through fuzz testing' list verbatim, ticket #65205, 'the release schedule remains as is...' verbatim, and the 'a plan will be shared for broader testing and continued iteration...' quote verbatim. source-2.html.gz (Make Core performance analysis): confirms author annezazu, the four storage strategies (post-meta RC2 baseline, custom-table, post-meta-transients, custom-table-with-transients), 'per-request REST dispatch time and database query counts during sustained 30-second polling windows' verbatim, 'first or tied-first on six of seven complete environments' verbatim, ~52% faster, the 'pathological transient code path that pushed dispatch latency past 26 ms' verbatim, and 'is the best option forward as the default for real time collaboration' verbatim; eight hosting environments confirmed. source-3.html.gz (Developer Blog May 2026, by Justin Tadlock, May 12): confirms RC3, May 20 target, the near-identical RTC-removal rationale quote verbatim, the new @wordpress/grid package, and the Templates/Template Parts REST endpoints 'include a date field' verbatim. source-4.html.gz (Gutenberg Times Source of Truth): confirms Gutenberg 22.0-22.6, the Breadcrumbs block, the Icon block + Icon Registration API, the 'Modern' replaces 'Fresh' default admin color scheme, color-coded visual revisions, navigation overlays, the WP AI Client / wp_ai_client_prompt() function, and the Connectors screen for OpenAI/Claude/Gemini. source-5.html.gz (Search Engine Journal, by Roger Montti): confirms the Mullenweg 'time DoS for critical people' quote in context of the WP Engine litigation, and the WordPress.com VIP/Enterprise controlled rollout since late 2025.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (May 20 release, May 8 removal, '12 days before', ticket #65205, the four storage strategies, 52%/37% speedups, 26ms latency, eight environments, Gutenberg 22.0-22.6, the block/API/admin additions) traces to a cited source. No hallucinated facts.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section frames the cut as 'the project chose a predictable ship date over its most-anticipated capability' — an evidence-based observation drawn directly from the sources, not editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. This is one of three colliding WordPress 7.0 submissions in this batch. #1268 (this one) and #1265 are both News pieces framed on the RTC cut; #1268 is the stronger of the two — it cites the dedicated performance-testing analysis and the Gutenberg Times tracker, sources #1265 does not use, and carries materially more detail on what still ships. #1265 is therefore being rejected as the weaker duplicate. #1266 is a separate Analysis piece framed on the AI Client architecture; that piece is being approved as well because it is a different category, a different lead, and cites distinct primary sources (the AI Client developer note, the December 2025 planning post) — it complements rather than duplicates this News report.
editor_notes.quote_audit: Rule 3 check passed. Every quoted passage was checked against the snapshots and reproduces source text verbatim with correct attribution. One quote ('Once the immediate release work is complete...') is rendered with a lowercase 'once' mid-sentence — a normal capitalization adjustment, not a paraphrase. The 'the release schedule remains as is...' quote is cut cleanly at 'if the schedule needs to change' with the bot's own words ('to accommodate unwinding the feature') correctly placed outside the quote marks. No paraphrase-inside-quote-marks, no fabricated or misattributed quotes.
recommendations: Add the official WordPress project domains and gutenbergtimes.com to the source allowlist — the only automated finding was an allowlist-coverage gap, not a content problem.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurately and richly sourced submission. APPROVE. The strongest of the two RTC-cut News submissions in this batch.
